Title: Diverged Plant Terpene Synthases Reroute the Carbocation Cyclization Path towards the Formation of Unprecedented 6/11/5 and 6/6/7/5 Sesterterpene Scaffolds

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a sesterterpene scaffold-generating enzyme for metabolic engineering and combinatorial biosynthesis of novel terpenoids; should be co-expressed with a geranylfarnesyl diphosphate synthase to supply GFPP. Engineering of active-site residues may tune product profiles, but mutagenesis often abolishes activity.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranylfarnesyl diphosphate (GFPP) through a class I terpene synthase mechanism into multiple sesterterpenes via early formation of 15/5 bicyclic intermediates (brassitetraenes A/B), followed by reprotonation and further carbocation rearrangements to unprecedented 6/11/5 and 6/6/7/5 scaffolds.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces novel sesterterpene scaffolds including brassitetraenes, caprutrienes, caprudiene, brarapadienes, and arathanadienes when expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana with AtGFPPS1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS metabolite profiling; large-scale production and isolation; NMR structural elucidation; time-course and D2O feeding experiments.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves; D2O feeding/time-course experiments for mechanistic interpretation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous transient expression in infiltrated leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cr089 belongs to the phylogenetically diverged clade IV of plant STSs. It generates products sharing a unified cyclization path diverted from previously characterized plant STSs by formation of 15/5 bicyclic intermediates and reprotonation-dependent rearrangement.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Clade IV sesterterpene cyclization branch leading to 6/11/5 and 6/6/7/5 scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I terpene synthase; plant sesterterpene synthase; contains DDXXD and NSE/DTE-type class I TPS motifs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cr089 is a newly characterized clade IV plant sesterterpene synthase. 2. When co-expressed with AtGFPPS1 in Nicotiana benthamiana, it converts GFPP into multiple unprecedented sesterterpenes through a diverted carbocation cyclization route. 3. Product analysis supports a mechanism involving early deprotonation to brassitetraene intermediates, reprotonation, and further cyclization/rearrangement.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Novel sesterterpenes (brassitetraenes, caprutrienes, caprudiene, brarapadienes, arathanadienes)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Infiltrated Nicotiana benthamiana leaves (heterologous production site)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural product structural diversity; potential new bioactivities; chemical ecology relevance.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A promising enzyme for pathway engineering of unusual sesterterpene skeletons. Can be paired with GFPP synthase and targeted mutagenesis of active-site residues to redirect product distribution; caution that many substitutions abolish activity.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GFPP into a mixture of sesterterpenes via class I ionization-initiated carbocation cyclization, including formation of 15/5 bicyclic intermediates and subsequent reprotonation-dependent conversion into 6/11/5 and 6/6/7/5 fused-ring products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces unprecedented sesterterpene products in Nicotiana benthamiana; Lys424 is implicated in promoting reprotonation required for efficient conversion of intermediate 2a into tetracyclic products 7 and 8.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; NMR-based product identification; D2O feeding/time-course analysis; site-directed mutagenesis (K424I); comparative product profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves; D2O feeding and harvest at 4, 5, 8, and 10 days post infiltration.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous transient expression in infiltrated leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtTPS17 is a clade IV plant STS. Mutation K424I retained about 68% activity but reduced conversion of intermediate 2a into tetracyclic products 7 and 8, supporting a role of Lys424 in reprotonation/general acid-base assistance. N354 and K424 were identified as clade IV-conserved active-site residues differing from clades I-III.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Clade IV sesterterpene cyclization branch leading to 6/11/5 and 6/6/7/5 scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Most tested active-site mutations caused strong loss of enzymatic activity, indicating limited mutational tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Brassitetraene A and tetracyclic products including arathanadiene A and B
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I terpene synthase; plant sesterterpene synthase; active-site Lys424 implicated in reprotonation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS17 is a clade IV plant sesterterpene synthase that generates unprecedented sesterterpene scaffolds from GFPP. 2. D2O feeding and time-course studies support a mechanism involving deprotonation to intermediate 2a/2b followed by reprotonation. 3. Lys424 is a key active-site residue influencing reprotonation and efficient formation of tetracyclic products 7 and 8.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Novel sesterterpenes (especially brassitetraenes and arathanadienes, plus related clade IV products)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Infiltrated Nicotiana benthamiana leaves (heterologous production site)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural product diversity and potential bioactivity reservoir.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be deployed in synthetic biology platforms to generate rare plant sesterterpene scaffolds when combined with GFPP supply. Suitable for protein engineering aimed at expanding terpene chemical space, but active-site substitutions may compromise function.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GFPP through a class I terpene synthase reaction into multiple novel sesterterpenes via a diverted carbocation pathway featuring deprotonation to 15/5 bicyclic brassitetraene intermediates and subsequent reprotonation-driven cyclization/rearrangement.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces multiple unprecedented sesterterpene scaffolds when co-expressed with AtGFPPS1 in Nicotiana benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; purification and NMR structural elucidation; D2O feeding/time-course experiments.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves; D2O feeding/time-course experiments.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous transient expression in infiltrated leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Br580 is a clade IV plant STS from Brassica rapa and shares the diverged cyclization logic of the other clade IV enzymes characterized in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Clade IV sesterterpene cyclization branch leading to 6/11/5 and 6/6/7/5 scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Most analogous active-site mutations tested in clade IV STSs caused loss of activity.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Brassitetraene A/B and downstream tricyclic/tetracyclic sesterterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I terpene synthase; plant sesterterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Br580 is a newly characterized clade IV plant sesterterpene synthase. 2. In Nicotiana benthamiana, it converts GFPP into multiple unprecedented sesterterpenes through a rerouted carbocation cyclization pathway. 3. Its products support a unified mechanism involving 15/5 bicyclic intermediates and reprotonation-dependent formation of complex fused-ring scaffolds.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Novel sesterterpenes (brassitetraenes, caprutrienes, caprudiene, brarapadienes, arathanadienes)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Infiltrated Nicotiana benthamiana leaves (heterologous production site)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural product structural diversity; potential bioactivities; chemical ecology relevance.
